title: INSERT — add rows
instruction:
`INSERT INTO table (cols) VALUES (...)` adds new rows. Insert multiple rows in one statement for efficiency.

Run this.
successMessage: All three rows inserted in one statement. `done` defaults to 0 (the column default). Batching inserts is more efficient than inserting one at a time.

title: UPDATE — modify rows
instruction:
`UPDATE` changes existing rows. Always include a `WHERE` clause — without it, every row is updated.

Run this.
successMessage: Only rows where `id <= 2` were updated. Task 3 remains `done = 0`. The WHERE clause is your safety net.

title: DELETE — remove rows
instruction:
`DELETE FROM table WHERE condition` removes matching rows. Without `WHERE`, all rows are deleted.

Run this.
successMessage: Two expired sessions deleted. Two valid sessions remain. `DELETE WHERE condition` is the safe pattern — always verify with a SELECT first.
